| Field | Value |

| **SEP** | 2243 |
| **Title** | HTTP Header Standardization for Streamable HTTP Transport |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Type** | Standards Track |

| **Author(s)** | MCP Transports Working Group |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#abstract"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Abstract</span>

<span data-as="p">This SEP proposes exposing critical routing and
context information in standard HTTP header locations for the Streamable
HTTP transport. By mirroring key fields from the JSON-RPC payload into
HTTP headers, network intermediaries such as load balancers, proxies,
and observability tools can route and process MCP traffic without deep
packet inspection, reducing latency and computational overhead.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#motivation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Motivation</span>

<span data-as="p">Current MCP implementations over HTTP bury all routing
information within the JSON-RPC payload. This creates friction for
network infrastructure:</span>

- **Load balancers** must terminate TLS and parse the entire JSON body
  to extract routing information (e.g., region, tool name)
- **Proxies and gateways** cannot make routing decisions without deep
  packet inspection
- **Observability tools** have limited visibility into MCP traffic
  patterns
- **Rate limiters and WAFs** cannot apply policies based on MCP-specific
  fields

<span data-as="p">By exposing key fields in HTTP headers, we enable
standard network infrastructure to work with MCP traffic using existing,
well-supported mechanisms.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#specification"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Specification</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#standard-headers"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Standard Headers</span>

<span data-as="p">The Streamable HTTP transport will require POST
requests to include the following headers mirrored from the request
body:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Header Name | Source Field | Required For |
|----|----|----|
| `Mcp-Method` | `method` | All requests and notifications |
| `Mcp-Name` | `params.name` or `params.uri` | `tools/call`, `resources/read`, `prompts/get` requests |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">These headers are **required** for compliance with the
MCP version in which they are introduced.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Server Behavior**: Servers that process the request
body MUST reject requests where the values specified in the headers do
not match the values in the request body.</span>

> <span data-as="p">**Rationale**: This requirement prevents potential
> security vulnerabilities and error conditions that could arise when
> different components in the network rely on different sources of
> truth. For example, a load balancer or gateway might use the header
> values to make routing decisions, while the MCP server uses the body
> values for execution. This requirement applies to any network
> intermediary that processes the message body, as well as the MCP
> server itself.</span>

<span data-as="p">**Case Sensitivity**: Header names (called “field
names” in
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#name-field-names"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 9110</a>) are
case-insensitive. Clients and servers MUST use case-insensitive
comparisons for header names.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-tools/call-request"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: tools/call Request</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="17" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: tools/call
Mcp-Name: get_weather

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "get_weather",
    "arguments": {
      "location": "Seattle, WA"
    }
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: resources/read
Mcp-Name: file:///projects/myapp/config.json

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 2,
  "method": "resources/read",
  "params": {
    "uri": "file:///projects/myapp/config.json"
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

</div>

####

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: prompts/get
Mcp-Name: code_review

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 3,
  "method": "prompts/get",
  "params": {
    "name": "code_review",
    "arguments": {
      "language": "python"
    }
```

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-other-request-methods"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: Other Request Methods</span>

<span data-as="p">For requests that don’t involve tools, resources, or
prompts, only the `Mcp-Method` header is required:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="17" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Method: initialize

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 4,
  "method": "initialize",
  "params": {
    "protocolVersion": "2025-06-18",
    "capabilities": {},
    "clientInfo": {
      "name": "ExampleClient",
      "version": "1.0.0"
    }
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-notification"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: Notification</span>

<span data-as="p">Notifications also require the `Mcp-Method`
header:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="9" language="http">

floating-buttons="true">

src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: notifications/initialized

  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
```

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#custom-headers-from-tool-parameters"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Custom Headers from Tool Parameters</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP servers MAY designate specific tool parameters to
be mirrored into HTTP headers using an `x-mcp-header` extension property
in the parameter’s schema within the tool’s `inputSchema`.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Client Requirement**: While the use of
`x-mcp-header` is optional for servers, clients MUST support this
feature. When a server’s tool definition includes `x-mcp-header`
annotations, conforming clients MUST mirror the designated parameter
values into HTTP headers as specified in this document.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#schema-extension"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Schema Extension</span>

<span data-as="p">The `x-mcp-header` property specifies the name portion
used to construct the header name `Mcp-Param-{name}`.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Constraints on `x-mcp-header` values**:</span>

- MUST NOT be empty
- MUST contain only ASCII characters (excluding space and `:`)
- MUST be case-insensitively unique among all `x-mcp-header` values in
  the `inputSchema`
- MUST only be applied to parameters with primitive types (number,
  string, boolean)

<span data-as="p">Clients MUST reject tool definitions where any
`x-mcp-header` value violates these constraints. Rejection means the
client MUST exclude the invalid tool from the result of `tools/list`.
Clients SHOULD log a warning when rejecting a tool definition, including
the tool name and the reason for rejection. This behavior ensures that a
single malformed tool definition does not prevent other valid tools from
being used.</span> <span data-as="p">**Example Tool Definition**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="19" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "name": "execute_sql",
  "description": "Execute SQL on Google Cloud Spanner",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "region": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The region to execute the query in",
        "x-mcp-header": "Region"
      },
      "query": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The SQL query to execute"
      }
    },
    "required": ["region", "query"]
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-geo-distributed-database"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: Geo-Distributed Database</span>

<span data-as="p">Consider a server exposing an `execute_sql` tool for
Google Cloud Spanner, which requires a `region` parameter.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Tool Definition**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="19" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "name": "execute_sql",
  "description": "Execute SQL on Google Cloud Spanner",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "region": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The region to execute the query in",
        "x-mcp-header": "Region"
      },
      "query": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The SQL query to execute"
      }
    },
    "required": ["region", "query"]
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Scenario**: A client requests to execute SQL in
`us-west1`.</span> <span data-as="p">**Current Friction**: The global
load balancer receives the request but must terminate TLS and parse the
entire JSON body to find `"region": "us-west1"` before it knows whether
to route the packet to the Oregon or Belgium cluster.</span>
<span data-as="p">**With This Proposal**: The client detects the
`x-mcp-header` annotation and automatically adds the header
`Mcp-Param-Region: us-west1` to the HTTP request. The load balancer can
now route based on the header without parsing the body.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Request**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="19" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: tools/call
Mcp-Name: execute_sql
Mcp-Param-Region: us-west1

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "execute_sql",
    "arguments": {
      "region": "us-west1",
      "query": "SELECT * FROM users"
    }
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-multi-tenant-saas-application"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: Multi-Tenant SaaS
Application</span>

<span data-as="p">A SaaS platform exposes tools that operate on
different customer tenants. By exposing the tenant ID in a header, the
platform can route requests to tenant-specific infrastructure.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Tool Definition**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="27" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "name": "query_analytics",
  "description": "Query analytics data for a tenant",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "tenant_id": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The tenant identifier",
        "x-mcp-header": "TenantId"
      },
      "metric": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The metric to query"
      },
      "start_date": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Start date for the query range"
      },
      "end_date": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "End date for the query range"
      }
    },
    "required": ["tenant_id", "metric", "start_date", "end_date"]
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Request**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="21" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: tools/call
Mcp-Name: query_analytics
Mcp-Param-TenantId: acme-corp

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 5,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "query_analytics",
    "arguments": {
      "tenant_id": "acme-corp",
      "metric": "page_views",
      "start_date": "2026-01-01",
      "end_date": "2026-01-31"
    }
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#example-priority-based-request-handling"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example: Priority-Based Request
Handling</span>

<span data-as="p">A server can expose a priority parameter to allow
infrastructure to prioritize certain requests.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Tool Definition**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="19" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "name": "generate_report",
  "description": "Generate a complex report",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "report_type": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Type of report to generate"
      },
      "priority": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Request priority: low, normal, or high",
        "x-mcp-header": "Priority"
      }
    },
    "required": ["report_type"]
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Request**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="19" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Mcp-Session-Id: 1f3a4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c-2d3e4f5a6b7c
Mcp-Method: tools/call
Mcp-Name: generate_report
Mcp-Param-Priority: high

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 6,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "generate_report",
    "arguments": {
      "report_type": "quarterly_summary",
      "priority": "high"
    }
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#header-processing"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Header Processing</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#value-encoding"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Value Encoding</span>

<span data-as="p">Clients MUST encode parameter values before including
them in HTTP headers to ensure safe transmission and prevent injection
attacks.</span> <span data-as="p">**Character Restrictions**</span>
<span data-as="p">Per <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#name-field-values"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 9110</a>, HTTP header
field values must consist of visible ASCII characters (0x21-0x7E), space
(0x20), and horizontal tab (0x09). The following characters are
explicitly prohibited:</span>

- Carriage return (`\r`, 0x0D)
- Line feed (`\n`, 0x0A)
- Null character (`\0`, 0x00)
- Any character outside the ASCII range (\> 0x7F)

<span data-as="p">**Whitespace Handling**</span> <span data-as="p">HTTP
parsers typically trim leading and trailing whitespace from header
values. To preserve leading and trailing spaces in parameter values,
clients MUST use Base64 encoding when the value:</span>

- Starts with a space (0x20) or horizontal tab (0x09)
- Ends with a space (0x20) or horizontal tab (0x09)

<span data-as="p">**Encoding Rules**</span> <span data-as="p">Clients
MUST apply the following encoding rules in order:</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Type conversion**: Convert the parameter value
    to its string representation:</span>
    - `string`: Use the value as-is
    - `number`: Convert to decimal string representation (e.g., `42`,
      `3.14`)
    - `boolean`: Convert to lowercase `"true"` or `"false"`
2.  <span data-as="p">**Whitespace check**: If the string starts or ends
    with whitespace (space or tab):</span>
    - Apply Base64 encoding (see below)
3.  <span data-as="p">**ASCII validation**: Check if the string contains
    only valid ASCII characters (0x20-0x7E):</span>
    - If valid, proceed to step 4
    - If invalid (contains non-ASCII characters), apply Base64 encoding
      (see below)
4.  <span data-as="p">**Control character check**: If the string
    contains any control characters (0x00-0x1F or 0x7F):</span>
    - Apply Base64 encoding (see below)

<span data-as="p">**Base64 Encoding for Unsafe Values**</span>
<span data-as="p">When a value cannot be safely represented as a plain
ASCII header value, clients MUST use Base64 encoding of the UTF-8
representation of the value with the following format:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="2" language="text">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
Mcp-Param-{Name}: =?base64?{Base64EncodedValue}?=
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">The prefix `=?base64?` and suffix `?=` indicate that
the value is Base64-encoded. Servers and intermediaries that need to
inspect these values MUST decode them accordingly.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Examples**:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Original Value | Reason | Encoded Header Value |
|----|----|----|
| `"us-west1"` | Plain ASCII | `Mcp-Param-Region: us-west1` |
| `"Hello, 世界"` | Contains non-ASCII | `Mcp-Param-Greeting: =?base64?SGVsbG8sIOS4lueVjA==?=` |
| `" padded "` | Leading/trailing spaces | `Mcp-Param-Text: =?base64?IHBhZGRlZCA=?=` |
| `"line1\nline2"` | Contains newline | `Mcp-Param-Text: =?base64?bGluZTEKbGluZTI=?=` |

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#client-behavior"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Client Behavior</span>

<span data-as="p">When constructing a `tools/call` request via HTTP
transport, the client MUST:</span>

1.  Extract the values for any standard headers from the request body
    (e.g., `method`, `params.name`, `params.uri`)
2.  Append the `Mcp-Method` header and, if applicable, `Mcp-Name` header
    to the request
3.  Inspect the tool’s `inputSchema` for properties marked with
    `x-mcp-header` and extract the value for each parameter
4.  Encode the values according to the rules in
    <a href="2243-http-standardization.html#value-encoding"
    class="link">Value Encoding</a>
5.  Append a `Mcp-Param-{Name}: {Value}` header to the request:

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#server-behavior"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Server Behavior</span>

<span data-as="p">When receiving a request, the server MUST reject
requests with `Mcp-Param-{Name}` headers that contain invalid characters
(see “Character Restrictions” in the
<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#value-encoding"
class="link">Value Encoding</a> section).</span> <span data-as="p">Any
server that processes the message body (not simply forwarding it) MUST
validate that encoded header values, after decoding if Base64-encoded,
match the corresponding values in the request body. Servers MUST reject
requests with a `400 Bad Request` HTTP status if any validation
fails.</span> <span data-as="p">**Error Code**</span>
<span data-as="p">When rejecting a request due to header validation
failure, servers MUST return a JSON-RPC error response with the
following error code:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Code | Name | Description |
|----|----|----|
| `-32001` | `HeaderMismatch` | The HTTP headers do not match the corresponding values in the request body, or required headers are missing/malformed. |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">This error code is in the JSON-RPC
implementation-defined server error range (`-32000` to `-32099`).</span>
<span data-as="p">**Error Response Format**:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="8" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "error": {
    "code": -32001,
    "message": "Header mismatch: Mcp-Name header value 'foo' does not match body value 'bar'"
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Validation Failure Conditions**:</span>

- A required standard header (`Mcp-Method`, `Mcp-Name`, etc.) is missing
- A header value does not match the request body value
- A Base64-encoded value cannot be decoded
- A header value contains invalid characters

> <span data-as="p">**Note**: Intermediaries MUST return an appropriate
> HTTP error status (e.g., `400 Bad Request`) for validation failures
> but are not required to return a JSON-RPC error response.</span>

<span data-as="p">**Custom Header Handling**:</span>
<span data-as="p">Custom headers (those defined via `x-mcp-header`)
follow the same validation rules as standard headers:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Scenario | Client Behavior | Server Behavior |
|----|----|----|
| Parameter value provided | Client MUST include the header | Server MUST validate header matches body |
| Parameter value is `null` | Client MUST omit the header | Server MUST NOT expect the header |
| Parameter not in arguments | Client MUST omit the header | Server MUST NOT expect the header |
| Client omits header but value is in body | Non-conforming client | Server MUST reject the request |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">When rejecting requests due to missing or invalid
custom headers, the server MUST return HTTP status `400 Bad Request`
with JSON-RPC error code `-32001` (`HeaderMismatch`).</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#rationale"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Rationale</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#headers-vs-path"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Headers vs Path</span>

<span data-as="p">This proposal mirrors request data into headers rather
than encoding it in the URL path.</span> <span data-as="p">**Advantages
of Headers**:</span>

1.  **Simplicity**: All widely-used network load balancers support
    routing based on HTTP headers
2.  **Multi-version support**: Easier to support multiple MCP versions
    in clients and servers
3.  **Compatibility**: Headers work with the existing Streamable HTTP
    transport design without changing the endpoint structure
4.  **Unlimited values**: Header values can contain characters that
    would require encoding in URLs (e.g., `/`, `?`, `#`)
5.  **No URL length limits**: Very long values can be transmitted
    without hitting URL length restrictions

<span data-as="p">**Advantages of Path-based Routing**:</span>

1.  **Framework simplicity**: Many web frameworks (Flask, Express,
    Django, Rails) have built-in support for path-based routing with
    minimal configuration
2.  **Logging**: URL paths are typically logged by default, making
    debugging easier

<span data-as="p">**Trade-offs and Framework Considerations**:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Framework | Header-based Routing | Path-based Routing |
|----|----|----|
| Flask (Python) | Requires middleware or decorators to extract headers before routing | Native support via `@app.route('/mcp/<method>')` |
| Express (Node.js) | Easy via `req.headers` but requires custom routing logic | Native support via `app.post('/mcp/:method')` |
| Django (Python) | Requires custom middleware | Native URL patterns |
| Go (net/http) | Easy via `r.Header.Get()` | Native via path patterns |
| ASP.NET Core | Easy via `[FromHeader]` attribute | Native via route templates |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">For frameworks like Flask that strongly favor
path-based routing, implementing header-based routing requires
additional code:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="9" language="python">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
# Flask example: Header-based routing requires manual dispatch
@app.route('/mcp', methods=['POST'])
def mcp_handler():
    method = request.headers.get('Mcp-Method')
    if method == 'tools/call':
        return handle_tools_call(request)
    elif method == 'resources/read':
        return handle_resources_read(request)
    # ... etc
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">Despite this additional complexity in some frameworks,
header-based routing was chosen because:</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Backwards Compatibility** introducing path based
    routing would require all existing MCP Servers to take a major
    update, and potentially support two sets of endpoints to support
    multiple versions. Even if the SDKs can paper over this additional
    operational concerns like testing, metrics, etc would need to
    happen. Header based routing requires minimal client side changes.
    And clients which don’t opt in will still function correctly.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Infrastructure benefits outweigh framework
    complexity**: The primary goal is enabling network infrastructure
    (load balancers, proxies, WAFs) to route and process requests
    without body parsing. This benefit applies regardless of the server
    framework.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#infrastructure-support"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Infrastructure Support</span>

<span data-as="p">HTTP header-based routing and processing is supported
by:</span>

- **Load Balancers**: All major load balancers (HAProxy, NGINX,
  Cloudflare, F5, Envoy/Istio)
- **Rate Limiting**: 9 of 11 popular rate-limiting solutions
- **Authorization**: Kong, Tyk, AWS API Gateway, Google Cloud Apigee,
  Azure API Gateway, NGINX, Apache APISIX, Istio/Envoy
- **Web Application Firewalls**: Cloudflare WAF, AWS WAF, Azure WAF, F5
  Advanced WAF, FortiWeb, Imperva WAF, Barracuda WAF, ModSecurity,
  Akamai, Wallarm
- **Observability**: Most observability solutions can extract data from
  HTTP headers

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#explicit-header-names-in-x-mcp-header"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Explicit Header Names in
x-mcp-header</span>

<span data-as="p">The design uses an explicit name value in
`x-mcp-header` rather than deriving the header name from the parameter
name because:</span>

1.  **Case sensitivity mismatch**: Header names are case-insensitive,
    but JSON Schema property names are case-sensitive
2.  **Character set constraints**: Header names are limited to ASCII
    characters, but tool parameter names may contain arbitrary Unicode
3.  **Simplicity**: No complex scheme needed for constructing header
    names from nested properties

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#placement-within-json-schema"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Placement Within JSON Schema</span>

<span data-as="p">The `x-mcp-header` extension is placed directly within
the JSON Schema of the property to be mirrored, rather than in a
separate metadata field outside the schema. This design choice offers
several advantages:</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Co-location**: The header mapping is defined
    alongside the property it affects, making it immediately clear which
    parameter will be mirrored. Developers don’t need to cross-reference
    between the schema and a separate metadata structure.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Established pattern**: JSON Schema explicitly
    supports extension keywords (properties starting with `x-`), and
    this pattern is widely used in ecosystems like OpenAPI. Tool authors
    and SDK developers are already familiar with this approach.</span>
3.  <span data-as="p">**Schema composability**: When schemas are
    composed, extended, or referenced using `$ref`, the `x-mcp-header`
    annotation travels with the property definition. A separate metadata
    structure would require complex synchronization logic to maintain
    consistency.</span>
4.  <span data-as="p">**Tooling compatibility**: Existing JSON Schema
    validators ignore unknown keywords by default, so adding
    `x-mcp-header` doesn’t break existing schema validation. Tools that
    don’t understand this extension simply skip it.</span>
5.  <span data-as="p">**Reduced complexity**: A separate metadata
    structure would require defining a mapping mechanism (e.g., JSON
    Pointer or property paths) to associate headers with properties,
    adding implementation complexity and potential for errors.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#scope-tools-only"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Scope: Tools Only</span>

<span data-as="p">The `x-mcp-header` mechanism currently applies only to
`tools/call` requests because tools are the only MCP primitive with an
`inputSchema` that supports JSON Schema extension keywords. Resources
and prompts lack an equivalent schema structure: `resources/read` takes
only a `uri` (already exposed via `Mcp-Name`), and `prompts/get` defines
arguments as a simple `{name, description, required}` array without JSON
Schema extensibility. Generalizing custom header mapping to these
primitives would require adding `inputSchema`-style definitions to
resources and prompts, which is a larger specification change. This is
noted as a potential future extension.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#no-specification-level-header-size-limit"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">No Specification-Level Header Size
Limit</span>

<span data-as="p">This specification intentionally does not define
limits on individual header value length, total MCP header size, or
number of custom headers. Headers are solely an HTTP concept, and HTTP
itself
(<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 9110</a>) does not specify header
size limits. Common HTTP infrastructure imposes its own limits — ranging
from 4–8 KB on some servers (e.g., Apache at ~8190 bytes) to 128 KB on
others (e.g., Cloudflare) — but the appropriate limit depends on the
deployment environment, which only the service operator can
determine.</span> <span data-as="p">Defining a specification-level limit
(such as “omit headers exceeding 8192 bytes”) would introduce
problems:</span>

1.  **Arbitrary threshold**: Any chosen value would be too low for some
    deployments and irrelevant for others. The “right” limit varies by
    infrastructure.
2.  **Counterproductive omission**: If a client omits a header because
    it exceeds a spec-defined limit, servers and intermediaries that
    rely on that header for routing must either parse the body or reject
    the request — undermining the core purpose of exposing values in
    headers.
3.  **Unnecessary SDK burden**: SDK maintainers would need to implement
    and test limit-checking logic for a constraint that rarely applies
    in practice.
4.  **Redundant with HTTP**: Servers and intermediaries already reject
    oversized headers using standard HTTP status codes
    (`413 Request Entity Too Large`,
    `431 Request Header Fields Too Large`), which clients must handle
    regardless.

> <span data-as="p">**Note to implementers**: Servers, intermediaries,
> and clients MAY independently impose limits on individual header size,
> total MCP header size, or number of custom headers as appropriate for
> their deployment environment. Servers SHOULD document any limits they
> impose. Clients SHOULD gracefully handle
> `413 Request Entity Too Large` or
> `431 Request Header Fields Too Large` responses. Tool authors SHOULD
> limit `x-mcp-header` annotations to parameters that provide clear
> infrastructure benefits.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#encoding-approach-for-unsafe-values"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Encoding Approach for Unsafe Values</span>

<span data-as="p">Four approaches were considered for encoding parameter
values that cannot be safely represented as plain ASCII header values
(non-ASCII characters, leading/trailing whitespace, control
characters):</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Sentinel wrapping (chosen approach)**: Use the
    `=?base64?{value}?=` prefix/suffix within the same
    `Mcp-Param-{Name}` header to signal Base64-encoded values.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Separate header name**: Use a distinct header
    name for encoded values, e.g. `Mcp-ParamEncoded-{Name}`, so the
    encoding is indicated by the header name rather than the value
    format.</span>
3.  <span data-as="p">**Implicit encoding**: Let the parser infer
    encoding from the tool schema, e.g. via a
    `"x-mcp-header-encoding": "base64"` annotation in the tool
    definition.</span>
4.  <span data-as="p">**Always encode**: Base64-encode every
    `Mcp-Param-{Name}` value unconditionally.</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----|----|----|
| Sentinel wrapping | Single header name per parameter; common case (plain ASCII) is human-readable; intermediaries can route on plain values without decoding | In-band signaling can theoretically collide with literal values; every reader must check for the prefix |
| Separate header name | No in-band ambiguity; encoding is self-documenting from the header name | Doubles the header namespace; every intermediary must check two header names per parameter; needs a conflict rule if both are present |
| Implicit encoding | Simplest wire format; no sentinels or extra headers | Intermediaries need access to the tool schema to know whether to decode — defeats the purpose of exposing values in headers; static per-parameter decision doesn’t handle the mixed case well |
| Always encode | Simplest rules; no conditional logic or ambiguity | Plain ASCII values become unreadable; intermediaries must decode Base64 to inspect any value, significantly undermining the core motivation of this SEP |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Conclusion**: The sentinel wrapping approach
provides the best trade-off. The primary use case for custom headers is
enabling intermediaries to route and filter on simple, readable values
like region names and tenant IDs — these are invariably plain ASCII and
never trigger Base64 encoding. Option 4 makes all values opaque to
intermediaries. Option 3 leaves intermediaries unable to distinguish
encoded from literal values without access to the tool schema. Option 2
eliminates in-band ambiguity but doubles the header namespace, requiring
intermediaries to check two possible header names per parameter and
adding a conflict rule when both are present. The theoretical collision
risk of the sentinel in Option 1 is negligible since `=?base64?...?=` is
an unlikely literal parameter value in practice.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#backward-compatibility"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Backward Compatibility</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#standard-headers-2"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Standard Headers</span>

<span data-as="p">Existing clients and SDKs will be required to include
the standard headers when using the new MCP version. This is a minor
addition since clients already include headers like
`Mcp-Protocol-Version`, adding only one or two new headers per
message.</span> <span data-as="p">Servers implementing the new version
MUST reject requests missing required headers. Servers MAY support older
clients by accepting requests without headers when negotiating an older
protocol version.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2243-http-standardization.html#custom-headers-from-tool-parameters-2"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Custom Headers from Tool Parameters</span>

<span data-as="p">The `x-mcp-header` extension is optional for servers.
Existing tools without this property continue to work unchanged.
However, clients implementing the MCP version that includes this
specification MUST support the feature. Older clients that do not
support `x-mcp-header` will still function but will not provide the
header-based routing benefits that servers may depend on.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#security-implications"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Security Implications</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#header-injection"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Header Injection</span>

<span data-as="p">Header injection attacks occur when malicious values
containing control characters (especially `\r\n`) are included in
headers, potentially allowing attackers to inject additional headers or
terminate the header section early.</span> <span data-as="p">Clients
MUST follow the <a href="2243-http-standardization.html#value-encoding"
class="link">Value Encoding</a> rules defined in this specification.
These rules ensure that:</span>

- Control characters are never included in header values
- Non-ASCII values are safely encoded using Base64
- Values exceeding safe length limits are omitted

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#header-spoofing"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Header Spoofing</span>

<span data-as="p">Servers MUST validate that header values match the
corresponding values in the request body. This prevents clients from
sending mismatched headers to manipulate routing while executing
different operations.</span> <span data-as="p">For example, a malicious
client could attempt to:</span>

- Route a request to a less-secured region while executing operations
  intended for a high-security region
- Bypass rate limits by spoofing tenant identifiers
- Evade security policies by misrepresenting the operation being
  performed

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#information-disclosure"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Information Disclosure</span>

<span data-as="p">Tool parameter values designated for headers will be
visible to network intermediaries (load balancers, proxies, logging
systems). Server developers:</span>

- SHOULD NOT mark sensitive parameters (passwords, API keys, tokens,
  PII) with `x-mcp-header`
- SHOULD document which parameters are exposed as headers
- SHOULD consider that Base64 encoding provides no confidentiality—it is
  merely an encoding, not encryption

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#trusting-header-values"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Trusting Header Values</span>

<span data-as="p">Header values originate from tool call arguments,
which may be influenced by an LLM or a malicious client. Intermediaries
and servers MUST NOT treat these values as trusted input for
security-sensitive decisions. In particular:</span>

- Header values that imply access to specific resources (e.g., tenant
  IDs, region names) MUST be independently verified against the
  authenticated user’s permissions before granting access to those
  resources.
- Header values MUST NOT be used as the sole basis for granting elevated
  privileges without server-side enforcement of rate limits and quotas.
- Deployments SHOULD reject requests with oversized or excessive headers
  early in the pipeline — before performing Base64 decoding or body
  parsing — to mitigate denial-of-service risks from crafted payloads.

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2243-http-standardization.html#conformance-test-cases"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>
